meta.name: Path Traversal
meta.description: Path traversal (directory traversal) payloads for Linux and Windows. Covers basic sequences, URL encoding, double encoding, null-byte injection, filter bypasses, and direct file reads.

payloads.description: Single-level traversal to /etc/passwd — starting point for testing
Two-level traversal — for apps that strip one level
Three-level traversal — typical for web roots inside /var/www/html
Four-level traversal — covers deeper web root paths
Five-level traversal
Deep traversal to /etc/shadow — contains hashed passwords (requires root)
Absolute path — works if the application allows absolute paths
Process environment variables — may contain secrets, flags, or db credentials
Current process command line — reveals application invocation and config
Windows traversal to win.ini — confirms path traversal on IIS/Windows
Three-level Windows traversal to win.ini
Windows hosts file traversal — confirms deep traversal on Windows
Absolute Windows path — tests if absolute paths are allowed
URL-encoded / as %2f — bypasses filters checking for literal ../
Fully URL-encoded .. and / — %2e=dot, %2f=slash
Mixed encoding — dots encoded, slash literal
URL-encoded backslash (%5c) for Windows traversal
Double URL-encoded / — %252f decodes to %2f then to / in two passes
Double URL-encoded .. and / — double decode bypass
Double URL-encoded backslash for Windows — %255c → %5c → \
Null-byte truncation — truncates .jpg extension, targets C-based path validation
Null-byte with .php extension bypass
URL-encoded null-byte (%2500 → %00 after decode)
Doubled dot-slash bypass — ....// → ../ after stripping one sequence
Extra dots bypass — some WAFs strip ../  but not ..../
Mixed slash bypass — backslash-forward slash combination
Unicode overlong / encoding (%c0%af = / in old UTF-8 decoders)
Unicode fullwidth solidus (／) bypass — U+FF0F looks like /
Absolute path with traversal — starts from known web root
PHP stream wrapper — reads and base64-encodes /etc/passwd via PHP filter
